Patch Set 1:

Ping reviewer.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

The patch looks good. I'm not sure why you replaced the variable $sleep with the value that it was set to "1".

Patch Set 1:

> The patch looks good. I'm not sure why you replaced the variable $sleep with the value that it was set to "1".

1) According to test logs, the real sleep time between two checks may be tens seconds, that is too longer than the expected 1 second. Using const value is more direct and less suspected.
2) There are limited used points for such "const" variable.

Is this a patch you want to land to master permanently or just for debugging?  If you want to land it permanently, then the D_LFSCK messages should be changed to use the standard console message format so they can also be printed to the console.

commit.subject: LU-6635 lfsck: more debug message for sanity-lfsck test_18e
commit.message:
LU-6635 lfsck: more debug message for sanity-lfsck test_18e

It is suspected that the wait_update() does not check the specified
system status per second.

Another suspected point is that the lfsck status has not been set
as phase2 by lfsck_post.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Change-Id: I96defb2d4b5794b0ec8b8d3d07b6a780128c92e4
